meta.description: Notes on words, details, and how I found my way into UX. How do Cortázar, content design, and jigsaw puzzles connect? Meet #GiraFlor.
meta.title: About me | Florencia Frumento, Content Designer and UX Writer

hero.title: More about me and GiraFlor
hero.claim: A few notes on words, details, and how I found my way into UX.

quote.body: "Let's say the world is a figure; it must be read. By reading it, let's understand generating it."
quote.author: Julio Cortázar,

leerMundo.title: Reading the world
leerMundo.p1Before: When I first read Cortázar, I discovered that a tiny decision could completely transform a story. A change in voice, a period, or an unexpected sentence could make you reinterpret everything you had just read.
leerMundo.p1Emphasis: That curiosity about how texts are built led me to study Literature.
leerMundo.p2: Over time, I realized that same fascination also had a place in technology and digital products. People read screens, forms, error messages, and entire flows: that is where I found UX.
leerMundo.p3Lead: I care about understanding what a user needs in a specific moment:
leerMundo.p3Body: the excitement of launching an online store, the nerves of making a transfer, or the wish to solve something in just a few clicks and move on with the day. I also pay attention to mismatches such as an unmet expectation, unnecessary friction, or a message that does not fit what someone is going through. Then I try to improve it.

disenarElegir.title: Designing is also choosing
disenarElegir.p1Lead: Sunflowers inspired several visual decisions in this portfolio:
disenarElegir.p1Body: the color palette, the gradients, some stylistic elements, and even its name, GiraFlor.
disenarElegir.p2Before: And why sunflowers? I have always been drawn to the way they orient themselves toward the light. Today I am also choosing a direction:
disenarElegir.p2Emphasis: moving closer to the challenges that excite me, the products I want to work on, and the spaces where I can create the most value.
disenarElegir.p3: Today I want to keep growing as a Content Designer and UX Writer, putting into practice the mix of language, observation, and product thinking that makes UX so exciting to me.

imagenCompleta.title: The full picture
imagenCompleta.p1Before: Outside work, there is a hobby that represents me quite well:
imagenCompleta.p2Before: They share a curious common ground with UX. You start with a set of disconnected pieces.
imagenCompleta.p2Emphasis: You observe, group patterns, form hypotheses, and little by little the full picture appears.
imagenCompleta.p2After: They also force you to look closely and discover details that would go unnoticed from afar.
imagenCompleta.p3Before: That is why I find the same thread running through many of the things I enjoy: how we interpret what we see, what patterns appear when we look closely, and the small decisions that change an experience.
imagenCompleta.p3Emphasis: At the core, it is about how we read the world.
imagenCompleta.p4: I invite you to explore my projects and, if you think we could work together, I would love to talk.
